channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Takeaways from AP's reporting on the thousands disappeared in Colombia, Peru and Paraguay
item.description: Thousands of people have disappeared in Latin America during decadeslong conflicts. Many have never been found, presumed to be the victims of dictatorships, insurgencies or organized crime.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Intelligence sharing by the US and its allies has saved lives. Trump could test those ties
item.description: Current and former European and U.S. officials have raised concerns about some of President Donald Trump’s picks for top intelligence posts.
item.dc:creator: Emma Burrows And David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel, Hamas reach deal to release hostages and allow Palestinians to return to Gaza Strip
item.description: An agreement has been reached between Israel and Hamas that involves the release of additional hostages and the return of Palestinians to the northern part of the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Belarus strongman wins a 7th term in an election the opposition calls a farce
item.description: The smiling face of President Alexander Lukashenko gazed out from campaign posters across Belarus on Sunday as the country held an orchestrated election virtually guaranteed to give the 70-year-old autocrat yet another term on top of his three decades in power.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 22 reported killed in Lebanon before agreement to extend deadline for Israeli forces to withdraw
item.description: MAYS AL-JABAL, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli forces in southern Lebanon on Sunday opened fire on protesters demanding their withdrawal in line with a ceasefire agreement, killing at least 22 and injuring 124, Lebanese health officials reported.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump wants Egypt and Jordan to take in Palestinians from Gaza. Here's why the idea is rejected
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's suggestion that Egypt and Jordan take in Palestinians from the war-ravaged Gaza Strip was met with a hard “no" Sunday from the two U.S. allies along with the Palestinians themselves, who fear Israel would never allow them to return.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Deal reached to release Israeli hostage and allow Palestinians into north Gaza
item.description: Mediator Qatar announced early Monday that an agreement has been reached to release an Israeli civilian hostage and allow Palestinians to return to northern Gaza, easing the first major crisis of the fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Moreno says he will introduce legislation to sanction Colombia
item.description: Sen. Bernie Moreno (R-Ohio) said Sunday he will introduce legislation to sanction Colombia this week after the country’s president reportedly denied entry to two planes carrying deported migrants from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Musk tells Germans to get over 'past guilt' at far-right AfD rally
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk told Germans they should be proud of their heritage and should “move beyond” the “past guilt” of older generations.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Belarus frees American woman from detention as strongman leader looks to extend 30-year rule
item.description: Belarus has “unilaterally” freed an American woman from detention, U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced Sunday, as the Kremlin-allied country held an orchestrated election poised to give strongman President Alexander Lukashenko yet another term on top of his three decades in power.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: About 70 people killed in attack on hospital in Sudan's Darfur region, WHO chief says
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Around 70 people were killed in an attack on the only functional hospital in the besieged city of El Fasher in Sudan, the chief of the World Health Organization said Sunday, part of a series of attacks coming as the African nation's civil war escalated in recent da
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 13 UN peacekeepers, allied soldiers dead in Congo as M23 rebels make gains in key city
item.description: The Rwanda-backed rebel group M23 is battling Congolese government forces in Congo's key city of Goma, causing the international airport there to be evacuated.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House says Colombia agrees to take deported migrants after Trump tariff showdown
item.description: The White House is claiming victory in a showdown with Colombia over accepting flights of deported migrants from the U.S., hours after President Donald Trump threatened steep tariffs on imports and other sanctions on the longtime U.S. partner.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Paraguay's disappeared: A dictator's shadow is a roadblock for justice, but a few keep up the fight
item.description: ASUNCIÓN, Paraguay (AP) — Despite being ousted in 1989 after a 35-year reign of terror, during which 20,000 people were tortured, executed or disappeared, some Paraguayans feel as if Gen. Alfredo Stroessner never truly left.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colombia's disappeared: As a coalition, former enemies now search for loved ones and dream of peace
item.description: Five decades of war among leftist guerrillas, right-wing paramilitaries, drug lords and the government in Colombia have left 450,000 people killed and more than 124,000 disappeared.
item.dc:creator: María Teresa Hernández, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Peru's disappeared: Dozens look for relatives lost to violence. A woman who knows their sorrow helps
item.description: The most violent period in Peru’s history still brings sorrow among the thousands who lost loved ones between 1980 and 2000.
item.dc:creator: María Teresa Hernández, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Holocaust Remembrance Day: A town once inhabited by Nazis reconciles with the past
item.description: As Holocaust Remembrance Day is marked on Jan. 27, a town in southwestern Germany unflinchingly confronts its past and reaches out to Jews.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio demands answers with 2 more Americans reportedly held by Taliban
item.description: Two Americans remain in Taliban custody after a two-for-one prisoner exchange the Biden administration negotiated before departing the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN refugee chief calls for more help for Syria as refugees begin to return en masse
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — The United Nations refugee chief said Saturday that some 200,000 refugees have returned to Syria from neighboring countries since the government of Bashar Assad was overthrown last month.
item.dc:creator: OMAR ALBAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas frees 4 female Israeli soldiers in exchange for 200 Palestinian prisoners as ceasefire holds
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Four female Israeli soldiers who were taken in the attack that sparked the war in Gaza returned to Israel on Saturday after Hamas militants paraded them before a crowd of thousands in Gaza City and handed them over to the Red Cross.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, SAM MEDNICK, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Auschwitz was liberated 80 years ago. The spotlight is on survivors as their numbers dwindle
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — The world's focus will be on the remaining survivors of Nazi Germany's atrocities on Monday as world leaders and royalty join them for commemorations on the 80th anniversary of the liberation of Auschwitz.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Opus Dei cardinal acknowledges Vatican sanctioned him after abuse allegation but denies wrongdoing
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — The once-powerful archbishop of Lima, Peru and the first-ever cardinal of Opus Dei acknowledged Saturday that the Vatican had imposed sanctions on him in 2019 following an allegation of sexual abuse, but he strongly denied any wrongdoing.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's what happens next during the ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: The first week of the latest ceasefire between Israel and the Hamas militant group is complete. Hamas has begun to release hostages and Israel has freed nearly 300 Palestinian prisoners. But the deal has hit its first major complication.
item.dc:creator: CARA ANNA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Auschwitz 80 years since liberation: Ryszard Horowitz's story of survival and making the American Dream
item.description: An exclusive interview with Auschwitz survivor and award-winning photographer Ryszard Horowitz, on his extraordinary life and his perilous childhood as a Jewish boy in Nazi-occupied Kraków.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fighting in Sudan's civil war sets ablaze the country's largest oil refinery, satellite photos show
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Fighting around Sudan 's largest oil refinery set the sprawling complex ablaze, satellite data analyzed by The Associated Press on Saturday shows, sending thick, black smoke over the country's capital. Forces loyal to Sudan’s military under army chief Gen.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US has not stopped military aid to Ukraine, Zelenskyy says
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Saturday the U.S. has not stopped military aid to Ukraine after newly sworn in U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced he would pause foreign aid grants for 90 days.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Argentina's Milei rails against wokeism, transgenderism in explosive speech at Davos
item.description: Argentinian President Javier Milei called out the radical left for imposing gender ideology on children, during a controversial speech at the World Economic Forum in Switzerland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A 2,000-year-old statue is found abandoned in a garbage bag in Greece
item.description: A marble statue believed to be more than 2,000 years old has been found in a garbage bag near the city of Thessaloniki in Greece; experts say the piece dates back to the Hellenistic era.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: Make products in America or face tariffs
item.description: “Come make your product in America, and we will give you among the lowest taxes of any nation on earth,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: 2 people are killed in a knife attack in Germany; Scholz says there must be consequences
item.description: A stabbing attack in Germany has left two people dead, including a 2-year-old boy, and three others injured; the suspect was arrested shortly after the attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tabloids apologize to Prince Harry, admit intruding on Princess Diana
item.description: Prince Harry claimed a monumental victory Wednesday as Rupert Murdoch’s U.K. tabloids made an unprecedented apology.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation
